controlBinding.rationale: Rule id(s) ALERT-004, ALERT-005, ALERT-009, MARROW-001, MARROW-002, MARROW-003 in modules/anemia/rules.json produce the required signal for DM-CBC-001 when evaluated by the anemia-assessment engine (src/engine.js assessPediatricAnemia), verified by tests/dangerous-miss-scenarios.test.mjs.

testMarkers: DM-CBC-001: multilineage cytopenia + blasts produces emergency-dominant alerts and a marrow-failure top candidate, not an isolated anemia diagnosis
DM-CBC-001 NEGATIVE: removing the multilineage/blasts findings removes the emergency dominance (proves the suite is not vacuous)

technicalExecution.note: DM-CBC-001 positive+negative assertions run against the live anemia-assessment engine and pass at the current baseline (185/185 total repository tests). This is repository-level technical/synthetic evidence only, never clinical adjudication or owner approval.

productCallers: src/app.js (imports and invokes assessPediatricAnemia from src/engine.js on form submit)
productIntegration.evidence: Verified live: index.html loads <script type="module" src="./src/app.js">, the shipped entrypoint. src/app.js imports { assessPediatricAnemia } from './engine.js' and calls it directly on form submission. src/engine.js's assessPediatricAnemia calls module.deriveFacts (modules/anemia/facts.anemia.js) then runRules (src/ruleEngine.js) against modules/anemia/rules.json. Every input field this hazard's control rule(s) key off is a declared property of schemas/patient-input.schema.json (patient, cbc, reticulocytes, symptoms, history, exam, labs, smear) -- the same schema the shipped form submits against. grep -rn "local-applicability" --include=*.js --include=*.mjs . (excluding node_modules) confirms src/app.js and src/engine.js do NOT import scripts/lib/local-applicability.mjs, so this row's reachability rests on the engine path only.

controlBinding.rationale: Rule id(s) HEM-003 in modules/anemia/rules.json produce the required signal for DM-HEME-002 when evaluated by the anemia-assessment engine (src/engine.js assessPediatricAnemia), verified by tests/dangerous-miss-scenarios.test.mjs.

testMarkers: DM-HEME-002: hemolysis markers with unknown reticulocyte response abstain from a unifying diagnosis
DM-HEME-002 NEGATIVE: a known high reticulocyte response resolves the pattern to strongly-supported (proves the abstention was driven by the unknown retic value)

DM-HEME-002 REGRESSION (P4-V1 R2): hemolysis markers positive WITH retic.response "low" (not "unknown") — the aplastic-crisis signature — produces a SILENT empty differential and no alert when history is uncaptured

technicalExecution.note: DM-HEME-002 positive+negative assertions run against the live anemia-assessment engine and pass at the current baseline (185/185 total repository tests). This is repository-level technical/synthetic evidence only, never clinical adjudication or owner approval.

coverageFinding.description: DM-HEME-002 (hemolysis_blood_loss_or_marrow_production_mismatch) is control_bound (HEM-003), and HEM-003 does correctly cover the fixture's own scenario (hemolysis markers present, reticulocyte response unknown -> abstain). But the MORE DANGEROUS branch of this hazard family has NO engine control: hemolysis markers positive (>=2 of high indirect bilirubin / high LDH / low haptoglobin) WITH reticulocytes.response == 'low' -- the signature of an aplastic crisis (e.g. parvovirus B19 on hereditary spherocytosis or sickle cell disease) -- yields NO candidate and NO alert unless history.knownChronicHemolyticDisease AND history.recentViral are BOTH already true (only PARVO-001 reaches it). Verified by direct rule tracing against modules/anemia/rules.json and reproduced against the live engine (tests/dangerous-miss-scenarios.test.mjs, 'DM-HEME-002 REGRESSION (P4-V1 R2)'): a first presentation, or a known patient whose history was not captured this encounter, falls through every differential rule (HEM-001/HEM-002 need retic.high; AIHA-001 needs DAT+; HS-001 needs spherocytes+DAT-; MARROW-001 needs multilineage cytopenia; MARROW-003/TEC-001 need organomegaly-absence+recent-viral) and every alert rule (ALERT-004/006/009 need findings this pattern lacks), producing a silent empty differential indistinguishable from routine incomplete workup -- false reassurance by omission on the most time-critical miss in this hazard family. Whether the engine should gain a history-independent safety net for this branch is clinical content authority this repository task does not hold; it is recorded here as an explicit, owned, blocking finding rather than fixed by inventing a rule.
coverageFinding.blockedOnTask: UNSCHEDULED, owner-held clinical-content decision -- NOT P4-T4: plan section 4 P4-T4 is 'Add V4 silent-mode and V5 human-factors protocol-through-adjudication contracts, including alert lifecycle, override, downtime, handoff, recovery, and equity' (implementation/human-factors owners) -- it does not cover this rule-content question. No task in docs/project_plans/implementation_plans/enhancements/arc-clinical-council-adoption-v1.md section 4 currently schedules whether a history-independent aplastic-crisis safety net rule should be added to modules/anemia/rules.json; that is clinical-content authority this repository does not hold and does not invent a task id for. Recorded as owner-held (pediatric-safety-owner, per pediatric_clinical_review.json seat PCR-HEME) pending the plan scheduling a task for it; NOT authored by this repository task.

controlBinding.rationale: EP5-T6 (2026-07-21) hardened ARCH §10 condition 2 from a soft abstention to a refusal. src/engine.js assessPediatricAnemia() now throws AgeOutOfSupportedRangeError (code AGE_OUT_OF_SUPPORTED_RANGE) before any rule evaluates when the age falls outside modules/anemia/module.json supportedAgeMonths and no covering cbc.localRanges are supplied, so no result object is produced at all. SCOPE-001/SCOPE-003 no longer fire for this scenario because assess() refuses before rule evaluation; they remain witnessed by tests/witness/alerts/ fixtures that supply local ranges. Verified by tests/dangerous-miss-scenarios.test.mjs.

testMarkers: DM-AGE-003: an age below the 6-month built-in floor with no local ranges fails closed -- assess() refuses to produce an assessment
DM-AGE-003 NEGATIVE: the same CBC at a supported age resolves a real threshold (proves indeterminate was driven by age, not the CBC values)

controlBinding.rationale: Fail-closed applicability blocker code(s) SPECIMEN_MISMATCH, ANALYZER_MISMATCH, METHOD_MISMATCH, UNIT_MISMATCH in scripts/lib/local-applicability.mjs force a fail_closed decision for DM-LAB-005 against the SYNTHETIC SYNTHETIC-reference-interval-profile profile, verified by tests/dangerous-miss-scenarios.test.mjs. COVERAGE BOUNDARY (P4-V1 R6): these four controlIds cover specimen/analyzer/method/unit identity mismatch only. The panic-threshold/critical-value sub-dimension of unit_specimen_method_or_range_mismatch (BLOCKER.CRITICAL_VALUE_MISSING, CRITICAL_VALUE_CONFLICT, CRITICAL_VALUE_NOT_ASSERTED, CRITICAL_VALUE_UNIT_MISMATCH, CRITICAL_VALUE_BOUNDS_MISSING in scripts/lib/local-applicability.mjs) is a separate, independently tested mechanism exercised in the P3 lane (tests/local-applicability.test.mjs + tests/fixtures/local-profile/negative-cases.json), not by this row or by any P4 dangerous-miss scenario. No P4 scenario in this matrix exercises a CRITICAL_VALUE_* path; this row's own coverage claim is scoped to the four controlIds listed above only.

testMarkers: DM-LAB-005: specimen/analyzer/method/unit mismatch against the SYNTHETIC reference-interval profile fails closed on all four independent dimensions
DM-LAB-005 NEGATIVE: a fully matching request against the same profile is applicable (proves fail_closed was driven by the mismatch, not the profile itself)

technicalExecution.note: DM-LAB-005 positive+negative assertions run against the live P3 local-applicability evaluator and pass at the current baseline (185/185 total repository tests). This is repository-level technical/synthetic evidence only, never clinical adjudication or owner approval.

productIntegration.evidence: Verified live 2026-07-20 (P4-V1 R1, independently found by lab-medicine and general-pediatrics): grep -rn "local-applicability" --include=*.js --include=*.mjs . (excluding node_modules) returns exactly three matches, all under tests/ (tests/dangerous-miss-scenarios.test.mjs, tests/local-applicability.test.mjs, tests/synthetic-profile-containment.test.mjs) -- ZERO production callers. src/app.js imports only './engine.js', './evidence.js', './algorithmExplorer.js'; src/engine.js imports only './ruleEngine.js' and './modules/registry.js'. Neither imports scripts/lib/local-applicability.mjs. Read in full: schemas/patient-input.schema.json -- the only input surface the shipped app (src/app.js via index.html) accepts -- declares exactly these top-level properties: patient, cbc, reticulocytes, symptoms, history, exam, labs, smear. It has NO specimen, analyzer, method, or unitCode property at any level, so the gated dimensions this control's blocker codes evaluate cannot be entered through the shipped input surface even in principle. control_bound here describes an isolated, repository-tested function only.

finding.description: DM-LAB-005 is labelled controlBinding.status: control_bound (SPECIMEN_MISMATCH, ANALYZER_MISMATCH, METHOD_MISMATCH, UNIT_MISMATCH) and evidence.technicalExecution.status: repository_test_executed, which is true of scripts/lib/local-applicability.mjs's evaluateReferenceIntervalApplicability in isolation but materially misleading about the shipped product: that function has zero production callers (src/app.js and src/engine.js do not import it) and schemas/patient-input.schema.json (the only input surface the shipped app accepts) has no specimen/analyzer/method/unitCode property, so this gate cannot fire against a real submission today. A reader of this matrix could otherwise conclude the shipped tool protects against specimen/analyzer/method/unit mismatch; it does not.

controlBinding.rationale: No engine exists in this repository that implements a control for DM-EQUITY-009: assessPediatricAnemia (src/engine.js) is a stateless pure function with no subgroup/equity evaluator and no alert-lifecycle (override/downtime/handoff/recovery) state machine. Verified directly against tests/dangerous-miss-scenarios.test.mjs, which asserts this fixture declares executionBinding.engine: not_yet_implemented rather than claiming coverage.

testMarkers: no executable engine exists; the fixture declares this honestly rather than claiming coverage
an executionBinding claiming not_yet_implemented without a blockedOnTask is schema-invalid

technicalExecution.note: No control exists to execute against DM-EQUITY-009. npm run check only executes the honesty-guard tests asserting the ABSENCE of coverage (fixture declares not_yet_implemented; a mutated fixture that drops blockedOnTask fails schema validation) — this is not hazard mitigation evidence.

finding.description: DM-EQUITY-009 (subgroup_or_access_failure) has no executable control in this repository: there is no subgroup/equity evaluator anywhere in the codebase, and assessPediatricAnemia (src/engine.js) does not accept or reason over any subgroup/access dimension. Equity limitation exposure and unsupported-generalization blocking (validation_plan.md required signal) cannot be verified by execution today.

finding.description: DM-WORKFLOW-010 (alert_override_downtime_handoff_or_recovery_failure) has no executable control in this repository: there is no alert-lifecycle/work-queue state machine (acknowledgment, escalation, override, downtime replay, handoff, recovery) anywhere in the codebase. assessPediatricAnemia (src/engine.js) is a stateless pure function evaluated once per run; the per-run rule-audit completeness property tests/dangerous-miss-scenarios.test.mjs verifies is a real but narrow adjacent signal, explicitly NOT alert-lifecycle coverage. PRECONDITION DEPENDENCY (P4-V1 R8), not a sibling gap of equal scope: generating a correct alert (the other nine hazards' controlBinding) is not the same as an alert being seen, acknowledged, escalated, or acted upon. Every one of the other nine hazards' clinical effect depends on this missing lifecycle layer existing; without it, an alert that fires correctly can still be silently lost to an unattended queue, a downtime gap, or a handoff, with the same dangerous-miss outcome as if the alert had never fired. Do not read the ten-row 'eight control_bound, two no_control_exists' or 'two of ten carried, not closed' summary as '80% mitigated' -- this finding's preconditionForHazardIds lists every other hazard in this matrix precisely to make that misreading structurally checkable, not just a prose caveat.
finding.blockedOnTask: arc-clinical-council-adoption-v1 P4-T4 (V4 silent-mode / V5 alert-lifecycle, override, downtime, handoff, recovery contracts) — see plan §4 P4-T4 and pediatric_clinical_review.json seat PCR-SAFETY finding PEDS-HF-001

productIntegration.evidence: No control exists in this repository to evaluate reachability for; see the row's top-level finding PAC-P4T2-002 for the coverage gap itself.

blockedStates.rationale: No implemented control exists in this repository for these hazard(s); repository readiness cannot be claimed for an unmitigated hazard family.

P4-V1 (the eight-lens council coordinator review of this matrix and the P4-T1 fixtures) has now run three times. Verdicts recorded so far from seven of the eight specialty lenses: methods FAIL→FAIL→PASS; safety PASS→(re-run)PASS; equity PASS; hematology FAIL→PASS; laboratory-medicine FAIL→PASS; general-pediatrics FAIL→PASS-WITH-FINDINGS; clinical-informatics PASS-with-one-HIGH-finding — plus a task-completion-validator gate FAIL→PASS. Declaring readiness_audit_complete for the program is the council coordinator's outcome to declare, not asserted by this repository on its own authority; this rationale records review history only.
No executable engine exists to produce qualifying runtime evidence for these hazard(s).
No named credentialed human approval has been attached to any hazard in this matrix (P2-T3/P5-T4 not yet executed).
Certification requires credentialed_review_complete and clinical_validation_complete first; neither is satisfied.
Release authorization is a separate organizational decision, always downstream of certification (plan section 2).
Production activation is a separate organizational decision, always downstream of release (plan section 2).
